name: SolidJS (Fine-Grained Reactive UI Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Ryan Carniato / SolidJS Team

auth.notes: Frontend UI framework; no auth concept

performance.notes: Consistently top benchmark performance; no virtual DOM, true reactivity, <5KB runtime

agent_readiness.notes:
Fine-grained reactive UI framework. import { createSignal, createEffect, createMemo, Show, For, Switch, Match } from 'solid-js'. const [count, setCount] = createSignal(0). createEffect(() => console.log(count())). const doubled = createMemo(() => count() * 2). JSX-like: . Control flow: 0}>

{count()}

. {(item) =>
  • {item}
    • }    . No virtual DOM — direct DOM updates. createStore for nested state. createResource for async data. Solid Start: full-stack meta-framework. Primitives: onMount, onCleanup, createContext.

gotchas: JSX expressions must be called as functions: count() not count — common beginner mistake
Destructuring signals loses reactivity: const {count} = store() breaks tracking
Different mental model from React — reactivity is compilation-based, not hook-based
Smaller ecosystem than React — fewer third-party components
Solid Start (meta-framework) is less mature than Next.js or SvelteKit
best_when: Performance-critical UI where React VDOM overhead is a concern
Small to medium apps wanting React-like DX with better performance
When bundle size and runtime performance are primary concerns
Teams comfortable with fine-grained reactivity model
avoid_when: Large teams needing React's ecosystem and component libraries
When Next.js/Nuxt.js features are needed (use meta-framework)
React-specific libraries that can't be ported
summary: SolidJS is a fine-grained reactive UI framework. createSignal, createEffect, createMemo, createStore. JSX without VDOM — direct DOM updates. <Show>, <For>, <Switch> control flow. <5KB runtime. MIT by Ryan Carniato. Type-annotated. Fastest benchmark results. Best for performance-critical UI. Different from React: must call signals as functions. Smaller ecosystem than React. Solid Start for full-stack.
